key_facts_mentioned: you keep doing this:Chat service unavailable — start or restart the Lorevox AI backend to enable responses.
Give me a brief status check in two sentences.
Just acknowledge that we are testing stability and keep it under three sentences.
This is a stress test for Lorevox.
Please respond briefly and do not go into interview mode.

When I think about family history, there are many overlapping threads involving places, memories, generations, tensions, reconciliations, routines, losses, and stories that were told and retold over many years.
Some memories are clear and vivid, tied to kitchens, living rooms, weather, holidays, trips, illnesses, and family gatherings.
Others are partial fragments that only make sense when placed next to names, dates, relationships, and places.
In a system like Lorevox, those fragments can become part of a larger timeline and archive, but only if the system remains stable enough to process long context without crashing or drifting.
We are testing whether the system can remain calm and bounded even when the input is large and mixed with reflective content, operational intent, and multi-generational detail.
Christopher Todd Horne was born in Williston, North Dakota, and has family lines connected to Bismarck, Las Vegas, New Mexico, Spokane, and other places.
There are memories involving Janice and Kent Horne, stories that need to be preserved carefully, and multiple children and sibling relationships that have to remain distinct.
The project is not just a chatbot but a memory archive and memoir system where identity, facts, chronology, and emotional texture all matter.
The system has to manage direct conversation, extraction, summary behavior, and operator guidance without confusing those modes.
It also needs to avoid repetitive questioning, avoid generic assistant drift, and avoid turning every turn into an interview prompt.
At the same time, the technical stack is under pressure because the language model, speech-to-text system, and text-to-speech components all compete for limited GPU memory.
The goal of WO-10M was to reduce that pressure by lowering generation caps, adding a VRAM guard before generation, improving CUDA OOM recovery, and preventing repeated extraction retries.
This paragraph is intentionally verbose to simulate a heavier real-world turn.
It includes reflective wording, personal history framing, system-level vocabulary, and enough length to exercise token accounting and guarded generation.
The correct outcome is stability, bounded behavior, and safe recovery.
